title: Negligent Misato Ruined Rebuild
wikitext:
[]: Negligent Misato Ruined Rebuild             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 3:55 am Eric Blair gave up on Rebuild when "Soryu" became "Shikinami". Reichu felt Beast Mode was "fucking retarded". I felt both overreacted in those cases; I thought the name change was nice and Beast Mode was one of the best things I'd ever seen. I now have a gripe that's just as silly as theirs. I already mentioned it in the silliness thread, but I believe this transcends cartoon slapstick into a flagrantly out-of-character scenario in which internal consistency is messed up not because of errors in animation, not because something looks so cool it's ostensibly OK if it defies the laws of physics, or because the story scenario diverged from the original plan, but for the sake of a joke that takes away from Rebuild 's tension anyway due to its out-of-place cartooniness. Naked Asuka kicks Shinji in the jaw for seeing her. Said Shinji falls down, hitting his head on the hard floor, and proceeds to groan, twitch, and drool, unable to get up or apparently move his lower body. A Misato of any merit would aid him, make sure he didn't have a concussion or any fractured bones, or at least ask him if he needed help. 1.0 's Misato would have helped him. Remember her concern for his wellbeing? Remember how warm she was toward him when he was in despair? Here she just smiles and talks to PenPen. Forget the fact that she was drunk. That's cold, negligent and feckless . "It's a cartoon; Shinji's not in any real trouble." No, this is a cartoon where people can and do sustain horrible injuries and die bloody deaths. Shinji's arms have to be bandaged up from his fight against New Matariel a bit later. It's in this same world that Shinji sustains a blow that clearly messes him up horribly. In the real world, Shinji's inability to move except for his frantic twitches would indicate something gravely serious. Even in the wacky, wonderful world of 2.0 , no sane person would see that and think everything's fine. I'm trying to hold out on my judgment until the film's actually subbed, but that scene pulled me out of the film against my wishes and wouldn't let me get back in. Misato's all serious now when they fight the Angel... She's looking out for the Childrens, right? Yeah! She'll help Shinji and the others if they're in any danger! If they're hurt, she'll come to their aid! That isn't the same Misato we see at the beer table earlier in the movie. There's a difference between showing two different sides of a character and having a schizophrenic commander who cares for your safety one moment and smiles nonchalantly at your violently spasmic paralysis the next. Yes, I'm overreacting. I know this, but I can't help it. If Misato's behavior is considered acceptable in the Wevaverse, or if Shinji's paralysis and twitching after two harsh blows to the head aren't things worth being concerned over in the Wevaverse, the Wevaverse is not a universe I care about. I suppose it's a deeply-rooted feeling that comes from my intense despair as a child at authority figures scoffing at what I perceived as terrible pain, danger, or agony. One time when I was ten years old I saw an episode of Growing Pains (one of my favorite shows at the time) in which, right before the end credits, Mike is allowed to drive his car, but after he walks out of the room, it's revealed that Jason and Maggie had stolen certain necessary parts of his car (the spark plug and something else) that it wouldn't run without. It was a one-off joke that was never mentioned again, but it bothered me magnificently and I kept hoping that they'd apologize in a later episode. I thought I'd grown out of being bothered by that sort of thing. I guess I didn't...

[]:             Quote Post by OKdesuKA » Thu Jul 16, 2009 4:09 am OKdesuKA wrote: You're exaggerating.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 4:14 am OKdesuKA wrote: OKdesuKA wrote: You're exaggerating.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jul 16, 2009 5:22 am Well, NAveryW's right about that in real life, but the truth remains that we're being trolled viciously.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Thu Jul 16, 2009 5:41 am Well, while I can understand disliking a slapstick comedy scene for what it is or being out of place (as was already done in the other thread), I don't like it when the characters are "blamed" for acting accordingly. It's this kind of "taking comedy too seriously" that gets you comments like "I hatez Asuka cuz she's a mean bitch cuz she always hits teh Shinji!"... -_- edit: That said, Shikinami actually comes off much more violent here. Kicking Shinji's legs out was hardly for comedic relief, and kicking that other student as she's playing her game wasn't in an all that humorous situation either.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Thu Jul 16, 2009 6:03 am

[]:             Quote Post by catinajar » Thu Jul 16, 2009 6:18 am OK, so in real life, that sort of thing would indicate major head trauma (I work for the ambulance service so noone needs to tell me about the severity of those symptoms), but let's face it, in real life if you told someone that giant humanoid beasts that look like robots are here to move humanity on to it's next level of evolution in accordance with some semi-religious pieces of paper found in the bottom of the ocean, you'd be carted off to the nearest mental institution... Obviously some aspects of Eva have their grounds firmly planted in reality, but in this scene I'm pretty sure that Misato is just reacting to childish squabbles and we're not really supposed to question whether Shinji is succumbing to some sort of petit mal seizure because he got a look at Asuka's gazooms... Just a thought, we're all allowed to take issue sometimes, eh?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Thu Jul 16, 2009 6:30 am You seem to be especially angry over this scene (Two threads worth of angry). Perhaps an angry naked red-headed chick kicked you in the face as a child?

[]:             Quote Post by Barong » Thu Jul 16, 2009 7:45 am It's a slapstick moment in an anime ... what the F are we talking about here?

[]:             Quote Post by Triaspia » Thu Jul 16, 2009 8:05 am maybe he was just a little winded, i dunno about you but I've been knocked on my rear end a couple of times (though not once by a naked red head) and could hardly move for a few minutes either that or he was just enjoying the view from a new angle (I havent seen the camrip yet, still waiting for the dub, i dont know the force he hit the ground with or anything so I'm only speculating here)

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Jul 16, 2009 8:19 am I think the point the OP was trying to make is not about feeling sorry for Shinji, but rather about that this moment of slapstick, with it's exaggerated ways, derails the character of Misato established in the previous movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Mari Mai » Thu Jul 16, 2009 8:27 am make sure he didn't have a concussion or any fractured bones, or at least ask him if he needed help. 1.0's Misato would have helped him.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 16, 2009 8:45 am Naw-kun you answered your own question in the other thread: Misato's a horrible guardian that's too drunk to care.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 10:01 am SaltyJoe wrote: I think the point the OP was trying to make is not about feeling sorry for Shinji, but rather about that this moment of slapstick, with it's exaggerated ways, derails the character of Misato established in the previous movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 16, 2009 12:45 pm Naw wrote: but it's still way too out of placed in the world 1.0 established in which Misato was a caring guardian who did what she could to make sure Shinji's AT Field stayed above 50.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 1:31 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I understand your point, Naw-kun, but I think you're looking into the scene too much. It's meant to be for the sake of comedy, not a serious scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Ingrod » Thu Jul 16, 2009 2:28 pm EVA is not Aliens, The Godfather: Part II, 2010: The Year We Made Contact, or Terminator Salvation. 1.- Is Japanese anime, with the Japanese anime humor style, not an american movie. 2.- Anno and his team break the fourth wall in many of their films, you see FLCL? The movie is directed for Ann and his team, ergo... 3.- The scene is on character, Misato was a horrid guardian in her house. In the series and here, but she can be serious and responsible in her work. Nothing more to see here.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 2:31 pm Ingrod wrote: EVA is not Aliens, The Godfather: Part II, 2010: The Year We Made Contact, or Terminator Salvation.

[]:             Quote Post by Lagann » Thu Jul 16, 2009 2:51 pm NAveryW I think you ,and a couple of other people, set yourselfs up not to like this movie.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 16, 2009 2:55 pm Lagann wrote: NAveryW I think you ,and a couple of other people, set yourselfs up not to like this movie.
